title: Pune murder case: Why not elope? Siya's lover reveals motive behind 'plot to kill' Ketan
content: A planned murder disguised as a trekking accident has been uncovered near Pune. Siya Goyal and her friend Chetan Chaudhary allegedly conspired to kill Ketan Agarwal, Siya's fiancé. Police suspect Siya feared family shame more than eloping with Chetan. Suspicious CCTV footage of a hooded figure at Lohagad Fort led investigators to the couple, who are now in custody.

title: Bhagwant Mann video row: Accused received Rs 10L from Punjab officials to fabricate clip, cops say
content: Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann faces a political storm over an alleged Rs 10 lakh deal for a forensic report on a viral video. The BJP accuses Mann of suppressing allegations of sacrilegious conduct and misusing state machinery. Gurugram Police have detained two individuals in connection with fabricating the report, with the CM denying the video's authenticity and calling it a conspiracy.

title: Watch: Heavy rain lashes Mumbai, Thane; landslide hits Malshej Ghat
content: Mumbai and its surrounding districts are grappling with heavy rainfall, leading to a minor landslide at Malshej Ghat and widespread waterlogging. Authorities have issued weather alerts, with some areas under a red alert. Despite disruptions like the closure of the Andheri subway, essential services like suburban train operations remain largely unaffected. The region braces for continued downpours.

title: 'Tujhe full freedom hai': Axar Patel's message to Rishabh Pant
content: Rishabh Pant's return to Delhi Capitals for IPL 2027 has been met with full backing from captain Axar Patel. Patel emphasized Pant will have complete freedom, stressing their long-standing mutual understanding.  He assured Pant there's no extra pressure, calling Delhi his home. Patel believes Pant's inherent class will shine through, encouraging him to enjoy his natural game.

title: Messi and Ronaldo share once-in-a-lifetime World Cup coincidence
content: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo have reached a remarkable statistical symmetry at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with both stars recording an identical 20-year and 6-day gap between their first and latest tournament goals. Messi moved ahead as the all-time World Cup top scorer with 18 goals, while Ronaldo became the first player to score in six editions of the tournament.

title: After 9 years, Chinese supercomputer beats America's in world's fastest supercomputer ranking
content: China's LineShine supercomputer in Shenzhen has topped the latest TOP500 list, becoming the world's fastest at 2.198 exaflops and ending America's reign since 2017. Remarkably, it runs entirely on homegrown CPUs—no Nvidia, AMD, or Intel GPUs—delivering a pointed message to Washington's chip export controls. But experts caution the win isn't an AI crown: LineShine ranked only fourth on AI-style benchmarks, and top US hyperscaler systems don't even enter.

title: China’s new 'eyes in sky': How KJ-3000 is building Beijing’s airborne surveillance edge
content: China's innovative KJ-3000 airborne early warning aircraft, derived from the Y-20B transport, represents a pivotal enhancement in aerial defense with its state-of-the-art digital radar and powerful capabilities. This strategic advancement strengthens China's already formidable surveillance ecosystem, which boasts over 60 KJ-500 aircraft. As the US military grapples with modernization issues, China's rapid advancements in airborne battle management are poised to shift the airpower landscape significantly.

title: ITR filing FY26: Why Form 26AS is important for tax returns & how to download it online
content: Form 26AS is an important document that provides taxpayers with a consolidated view of tax related information linked to their Permanent Account Number (PAN).

title: DDA flats on offer again: Who should apply, what to check before booking
content: Delhi's aspiring homeowners can take advantage of two schemes introduced by the DDA, aimed at making housing accessible to different income levels. The DDA Nagrik Awaas Yojana and Towering Heights Karkardooma Housing Scheme offer flats in desirable locations such as Narela and Siraspur. With registrations open until June 30, these programs promise a fair, first-come, first-served allocation process to help individuals fulfill their housing dreams.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
content: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

title: US citizenship may cost up to 80% more: Who can apply, what changes and how Indians may be impacted
content: The Department of Homeland Security is considering a drastic 80% increase in fees for US naturalization applications, which could make citizenship unaffordable for many. If implemented, the proposal would also remove fee waivers and discounts for low-income applicants, likely affecting a significant number of Indian citizens among new Americans, whose growth has recently slowed. The public is invited to comment on these proposals within 60 days.

title: Home loan guide: How banks approve, process and disburse your loan
content: Securing a home loan involves a structured process, beginning with an eligibility assessment based on your financial standing, credit score, and income stability. Following application and fee payment, lenders meticulously verify your details and the property's legal and technical aspects. Once sanctioned, a loan agreement is signed, leading to disbursal, either in full for ready properties or in stages for under-construction ones.

title: North Korea bets big on navy with first destroyer; Kim issues 'brink of nuclear war' warning
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has unveiled his strategic vision to build two 5,000-ton warships over the next five years, indicating a notable enhancement in the country's naval capabilities. He reiterated that the naval nuclearization initiative is on track, stressing the pressing necessity for a modern naval base to counter perceived threats from the US and South Korea, reinforcing North Korea's commitment to nuclear deterrence.

title: Claude Code creator Boris Cherny says 100% AI coding is becoming ‘problematic’
content: AI's role in coding is evolving, with Anthropic's Boris Cherny admitting that 100% AI-generated code presents challenges for companies focused on ROI.  He highlighted that the true bottleneck is now 'good ideas,' not just code output.  Cherny also foresees a shift from manual prompting to 'loop engineering,' where AI agents self-refine tasks, though this raises cost concerns. Anthropic offers tools to manage these expenses for enterprise clients.

title: Olivia Jean files divorce complaint against Jack White after four years of marriage
content: Rockstar Jack White is facing his third divorce, with wife Olivia Jean filing for separation after four years of marriage. Citing "inappropriate marital conduct" as grounds, Jean is seeking alimony, life insurance benefits, and health insurance payments from the musician. The couple's surprise wedding in 2022, which took place on stage during a Detroit concert, has now culminated in this legal proceeding.

title: R Madhavan's wife, son express happiness as he gets honoured with a Padma Shri
content: Actor R Madhavan has been honored with the prestigious Padma Shri, a significant milestone in his career. Celebrated by his family, including wife Sarita and son Vedaant, Madhavan expressed deep gratitude for the recognition. He dedicated the award to cinema, his colleagues, and his family, acknowledging the unwavering support he has received throughout his journey. Numerous Bollywood stars also extended their congratulations to the versatile actor.

title: 'Subah ka bhula ... ': Axar Patel's hilarious welcome for Rishabh Pant
content: Rishabh Pant has sensationally returned to his original IPL home, Delhi Capitals, in a blockbuster trade deal.  Captain Axar Patel humorously welcomed him back with a classic Hindi proverb, "If someone who loses his way in the morning returns home by evening, he is not considered lost." Pant's move back to DC, where he is the all-time leading run-scorer, marks a significant shift after his stint with Lucknow Super Giants.

title: Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout
content: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively’s bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star’s wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions.

title: Ronaldo vs Messi at FIFA World Cup 2026: CR7 says Portugal vs Argentina clash would be 'top''
content: Cristiano Ronaldo ended a difficult week with two goals as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0, topping Group K at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old became the first player to score in six men's World Cups. After the match, Ronaldo called a potential showdown with Lionel Messi "awesome," a clash that could materialise in the quarterfinals if Portugal secure top spot in their group against Colombia on Saturday.

title: Pune businessman murder: CCTV footage, hoodie, Bali plan and 'snake' story emerge as key clues
content: A real estate businessman's death at Lohagad Fort, initially deemed an accident, was unraveled by CCTV footage showing a suspicious man in a hoodie. Police discovered the victim's fiancée and the hooded man, her alleged lover, conspired to push him to his death. The plot involved multiple attempts, a missing passport, and a motive rooted in a secret relationship and reluctance to elope.

title: 'Papa…': Final call of 10-yr-old abducted from pavement; father chased cab driver with stick
content: A 10-year-old girl was abducted and murdered by a cab driver in South Delhi after being pulled from a pavement where her family was sleeping. Her father recounted the harrowing moments of her final cry, 'Papa...', as he desperately tried to save her. The family, migrants seeking a better life, had been forced onto the streets due to financial hardship.

title: Insurance firm sat on accident claim for 9 years, told to pay full vehicle value
content: A car owner has won over Rs 4 lakh after an insurance company delayed settling his accident claim for nearly a decade. The West Bengal state consumer commission held ICICI Lombard liable for deficiency in service, ordering them to pay the full insured value plus Rs 1 lakh compensation.

title: Meet Ayush Singh: 19-year-old who built AI business earning Rs 1 crore a month
content: A teenager's remarkable Rs 1 crore monthly earnings from an AI venture have ignited widespread discussion. Ayush Singh, who overcame financial hardship during COVID-19, taught himself machine learning and built a successful business. His journey, highlighted by his mentor, underscores the untapped potential within many individuals to monetize their skills, prompting both inspiration and self-reflection among social media users.

title: Trisha’s birthday post for Vijay charms fans; Charmme Kaur fuels rumours
content: A recent Instagram comment by producer Charmme Kaur, calling actors Vijay and Trisha a "Pure gold couple," has ignited fresh speculation about their relationship. This remark followed Trisha's heartfelt birthday post for Vijay, which itself ended days of online chatter. Fans are buzzing with the possibility that Kaur's comment hints at a deeper connection between the popular stars, adding another layer to their enduring on-screen chemistry and off-screen bond.

title: Why ONGC wants a stake in two Venezuela oilfields — and needs US approval
content: Venezuela's oil industry has witnessed a prolonged decline due to a combination of depressed oil prices, economic mismanagement and US sanctions. During this period, PDVSA's operational capabilities have also weakened considerably.

title: India tears into Pak over 'unwarranted remarks' at UN: 'J&K was, is & will remain internal matter'
content: India has firmly rejected Pakistan's attempt to politicize a UN forum by raising the Kashmir issue. Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish stated that Jammu and Kashmir is an internal matter for India, emphasizing that such discussions are inappropriate. He also highlighted India's push for reviewing UN Security Council mandates for greater efficiency, aligning with the UN-80 framework.

title: Qatar eyes LNG output recovery within weeks, easing fears of wider Iran war energy shock
content: Qatar anticipates resuming full liquefied natural gas production within weeks, signaling progress in US-Iran peace talks crucial for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Attacks in early March had disrupted a fifth of Qatar's LNG capacity. Prime Minister Al-Thani emphasized a direct US-Iran hotline as vital for maritime stability and preventing future shipping threats, with global gas markets eagerly awaiting Qatar's restored exports.

title: 'Ye 12-15 saal khelenge': Goenka's old prediction on Rishabh Pant goes viral
content: Sanjiv Goenka's bold 2025 prediction about Rishabh Pant's future with Lucknow Super Giants has resurfaced after the star wicketkeeper-batter's ₹15 crore trade back to Delhi Capitals. Goenka had envisioned Pant leading LSG to multiple IPL titles over 12-15 years, placing him alongside legends Dhoni and Rohit. However, Pant's two-season stint saw mixed results, culminating in this surprising player swap ahead of IPL 2027.

title: 50 stab wounds, 3 deaths, bloodied staircase: How Bengaluru techie butchered her own family
content: A shocking triple murder unfolded in Bengaluru when a techie and her live-in partner allegedly killed her parents and sister over a Rs 30 lakh loan dispute. The couple, who had kept their relationship secret, reportedly stabbed the victims multiple times before escaping from their apartment. The father, in his dying declaration, revealed the loan issue and his desire for his daughter to return home.

title: Crisil sees office REIT portfolio growing to 190-195 MSF by FY27
content: Indian office REITs are poised for significant expansion, projecting a 25-30% increase in leasable space by next fiscal year, largely through acquisitions. Strong demand from flexible workspace operators and financial institutions is expected to maintain high occupancy rates of 92-93%. This robust demand, coupled with increasing rents, will likely keep profit margins healthy at around 70%, even as debt is used to fund growth.

title: Why Russia is suddenly building giant shelters for bombers that once sat in the open
content: In a significant departure from Cold War practices, Russia is now building extensive protective shelters at Engels Air Base for its strategic bombers. This initiative is a direct response to recent drone attacks that exposed vulnerabilities within their airbase operations. With an aging fleet of bombers, Russia is focusing on ground survivability to ensure its strategic deterrent capabilities remain effective in contemporary conflict.

title: In a first, US Senate votes to halt Trump’s Iran war; President fumes, calls move 'meaningless'
content: In a groundbreaking decision, the US Senate has passed a resolution that requires congressional consent for any further military action against Iran. This marks a pivotal moment in challenging President Trump's military strategy. Although the vote was close and a presidential veto is anticipated, the legislation reflects increasing anxiety regarding the rationale and economic repercussions of the ongoing conflict.

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT recommends Trust revamp; findings may be placed before CM
content: A Special Investigation Team has submitted its initial findings to the Uttar Pradesh government regarding allegations of embezzlement of donations for the Ayodhya Ram temple. The probe, initiated by the temple trust, is actively continuing as investigators gather more information. Officials confirmed the preliminary report was handed over to the Additional Chief Secretary (Home) by the Lucknow Divisional Commissioner, with further details expected as the investigation progresses.

title: Mamdani-backed candidates shake up New York politics, oust two sitting congressmen
content: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani's chosen candidates achieved notable victories in the primaries, significantly empowering the progressive faction within the Democratic Party. These triumphs included surprising defeats of incumbents and a crucial win in a pivotal congressional district, indicating a transformation in New York's political dynamics. Mamdani's focus on electing 'better Democrats' committed to the working class contrasts with establishment leaders who minimize the overall impact on the party.

title: Child's future unsafe with mother, Pune court awards custody to father
content: A Pune court granted interim custody of a minor boy to his Singapore-based father, citing the mother's defiance of court orders and obstruction of access. The child was brought to India by the mother without informing the father, who had secured a custody order from Singapore. The judge emphasized the mother's non-compliance and the child's distress, stating that court orders must be respected for the child's welfare.

title: No full refund for buyer who defaulted on property sale pact: HC
content: The High Court held that the purchaser himself had defaulted in performing his obligations under the agreement and was therefore entitled only to the amount payable after deduction of 10% of the advance money, as specifically provided under the contract.

title: Rishabh Pant's three-word message to LSG after return to Delhi Capitals
content: Rishabh Pant has returned to Delhi Capitals in a significant trade deal, exchanging places with spinner Kuldeep Yadav who moves to Lucknow Super Giants. Pant's two-year stint with LSG, marked by struggles and disappointing team performances, concluded with a simple "Thank you team" message. This move sees Pant take a pay cut but reunites him with his former franchise after a challenging period.

title: Timer on Earth's plants? New scientific model finally has a timeline
content: In a surprising twist to our understanding of Earth's future, new research suggests that plant life could thrive on our planet for another 1.8 billion years. While the sun’s heat poses a gradual threat, the resilience of the carbon cycle and plant adaptability paints a hopeful picture. Advanced modeling techniques reveal a longer era of lush greenery before extinction eventually takes its toll.

title: Indian student headed to US sent back from Amsterdam, unaware his F-1 visa had been revoked
content: An Indian student faced a shock in Amsterdam when US Customs and Border Protection denied him entry to the US, stating his F-1 visa lacked validity. Despite his university confirming his I-20 and SEVIS record were active, his visa had been revoked months prior. He was forced to return to India and advised to reapply for a new visa, being transparent about the revocation during the interview.

title: Bengaluru court re-issues non-bailable warrant against actor Prakash Raj
content: Complainant has alleged inthat besides being enrolled as a voter in the Shantinagar assembly segment in Bengaluru, Prakash Raj's name figured at two places in Chennai at the Velachary assembly constituency, and also in the Serilingam Pally assembly segment in Telangana.

title: Bronze statue of Maharishi Sushruta made in Swamimalai unveiled at Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh
content: The statue was made at Swamimalai in Tamil Nadu by a team led by highly respected sculptor and master craftsman Ragavanantham Sthapathi

title: Rasimanal is a perfect alternative to Mekedatu, says veteran engineer
content: The veteran engineer, who addressed a letter to Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay a few weeks ago, giving a brief outline of the project, suggests that the proposed dam project be implemented as a joint venture of the two basin States.

title: Return enumeration forms or risk losing voter status: Hyderabad District Election Officer
content: The month-long revision process, beginning June 25, will also involve polling station rationalisation and verification of voter records ahead of the publication of revised electoral rolls

title: Watch: Pune fort murder: Police allege Fiancée, lover pushed man into gorge
content: What was first reported as an accidental fall at Pune’s Lohagad Fort has now turned into a murder investigation

title: Businessman, 13 others held for birthday bash on Brahmaputra bridge
content: Police also seized four high-end vehicles used for the midnight event to celebrate the birthday of Guwahati-based businessman Gautam Baruah’s wife

title: When Chennai’s Veeranam drinking water project hogged the headlines for all the wrong reasons
content: Decades ago, the Veeranam project, meant for bringing water to Chennai, had continued to evoke controversy for long

title: Kerala HC invalidates oaths of 20 Thiruvananthapuram councillors, orders fresh swearing-in
content: High Court holds that councillors’ original oaths in the names of various deities, martyrs, and political movements in their swearing-in ceremony in January are invalid under Kerala Municipality Act, 1994

title: Glass ceiling: ‘Visibility is not equality, symbolism is not power’
content: Though women’s vote is more important than it ever was earlier, it hasn’t turned into increased representation, say women parliamentarians

title: Assam on high alert after flash floods in Arunachal Pradesh
content: Lower Subansiri district in Arunachal Pradesh recorded 72.8 mm of rainfall during the last 24 hours, damaging houses and infrastructure

title: India slams Pakistan for 'unwarranted' remarks on J&K at UNSC’s Arria-formula meeting
content: Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish asserted that the Union Territory is a matter "strictly internal" to India

title: India eyes 100GW as global hydropower shifts to ‘water battery’: Report
content: The 2026 Global Hydropower Outlook says China continues to dominate global hydropower development, with more than 300GW, including 218GW of pumped storage, under construction

title: Regional parties assert independence, stress national interest
content: Rajya Sabha members Javed Ali Khan (Samajwadi Party), Santrupt Misra (Biju Janata Dal) and former BRS Parliamentary leader K.R. Suresh Reddy speak about raising issues of regional interest at the national level and the idea of holding simultaneous elections for both Parliament and State Assemblies

title: Why West Bengal’s decision of linking social security benefits to SIR is being challenged | Explained
content: The linking of the Public Distribution System (PDS) and Annapurna Yojana to the outcome of the Special Intensive Revision in West Bengal was challenged by an independent trade union

title: CUET results declared: what are the next steps for students?
content: Since 2022, central universities have been conducting admissions based on CUET scores, rather than the earlier process of admitting students on the basis of CBSE results

title: ICAI CA Intermediate 2026 results declared; Shardul Vichare from Dombivali secures AIR 1 with 88.5%
content: A total of 1,53,862 candidates appeared for the May 2026 exams held across 611 centres in India and abroad

title: BJP govt trying to impose vegetarianism in Bengal: O'Brien
content: Throw eggs at rivals. But deprive children of nutrition by taking eggs off from midday meals. Imposing vegetarianism. Bengal rejects this, says Trinamool Congress (TMC) joint secretary Derek O'Brien

title: Telangana CM finds fault with leaders negligent towards SIR; process set to begin on June 25
content: Revanth Reddy says Congress would monitor the performance of leaders concerned for 10 days after SIR begins

title: India panel proposes simulator-heavy pilot licence to ease crew shortage, document shows
content: Airlines have been asked to respond to ‌the plan, after which the committee is likely to submit a final report to ​the head of the regulator

title: Ujjain land 'scam': Congress's allegations against CM Mohan Yadav baseless, says BJP
content: The Opposition Congress has termed the alleged land scam in Ujjain as "plunder of Mahakal's land" and demanded the resignation of Chief Minister Mohan Yadav

title: Kerala CM proposes inter-State collaboration with Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry to combat drug smuggling
content: V.D. Satheesan writes to his counterparts in Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry to join forces sharing to dismantle criminal enterprises that thrive on drug smuggling through joint enforcement and intelligence

title: Explained: Why did Delhi High Court quash charges against NewsClick?
content: The FIR alleged that NewsClick had received an investment of $1.5 million from Worldwide Media Holdings LLC, a Delaware-based entity in the United States, at an inflated share valuation

title: TVK government is clear on political opposition to and administrative cooperation with the Centre, says Vijay
content: He reaffirms his party remains ideologically opposed to the ruling party at the Centre, while making it clear that it would not oppose the Centre blindly

title: Pune trekker murder: CCTV clue, a hoodie in 33°C lead police to killers
content: Police found it unusual that the man was wearing a hoodie despite the temperature being around 33 degrees Celsius

content: I think he decided to take revenge on that day for that humiliation he had been forced to suffer and now he is busy with the 'todh-phod ki rajniti', says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: Bombay High Court says shared auto not 'workplace' under PoSH Act, sets aside ICC order
content: The Court, in its June 22 order, set aside the findings of an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that had held a State Bank of India (SBI) employee guilty of sexual harassment based on an incident that occurred during such travel

title: Between visibility and storytelling: Huma Qureshi and Kritika Kamra on Bollywood’s future
content: Upcoming actors struggle for opportunities because Hindi film production has fallen by half, says Ms. Qureshi; we are making movies for numbers and mid-budget movies have disappeared, says Ms. Kamra

title: The goddess of instant gratification
content: Was the Nimishamba temple built when the first wave of settlers came? Or was it in the second wave when the company induced them to stay?

title: AMMA crisis: Actor Shwetha Menon alleges ‘some vested interests stopped us from probing their wrongdoings’
content: She states in a Facebook post that ‘the accounts of the past two terms, including ours, should be reviewed, and a forensic audit is necessary for transparency and accountability’

title: Andhra Pradesh strikes gold with Jonnagiri mine, eyes more projects
content: Commercial production begins at Jonnagiri in Kurnool district as the State prepares to auction several gold-bearing blocks in Chittoor and Anantapuramu to boost investment and employment

title: Discovery of new moth genus in Kerala’s Idukki highlights Western Ghats’ hidden biodiversity
content: Known only from Idukki, the species of tiger moth, named Antaram idukki, underscores the need to conserve biodiversity-rich habitats in Western Ghats, say researchers

title: Cockroach infestation, expired FSSAI licence among hygiene violations flagged at Hyderabad’s Zepto warehouse
content: Rameshwaram Cafe, SVM Grand, Platform 65, and Iguru Restaurant found having hygiene and contamination risks

title: Probe on to identify Karnataka BJP MLAs who cross-voted; ‘will be punished’, says high command
content: Strong concerns were raised over lack of discipline in the State BJP at a meeting convened by party national president Nitin Nabin

title: "Heart Found Its Home": Pune Woman's Romantic Posts With Fiance Before Killing Him
content: On May 19, Siya had posted her 'birthday countdown' story on her social media as she and Ketan danced and twirled to a romantic song.

title: From Ports To AI Data Centres: Gautam Adani's Roadmap For India's Growth
content: Speaking at the group's Annual General Meeting (AGM), Gautam Adani said infrastructure and intelligence will be the two key pillars shaping India's future.

title: Anthropic's Mythos Model Found Vulnerabilities In Classified US Government Systems
content: A official said Anthropic had teamed up with US intelligence agencies to conduct tests using the company's Mythos model.

title: "Going To Happen": UN Watchdog Chief On Inspection Of Iran's Nuclear Sites
content: The comment by International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Mariano Grossi was the firmest yet from the United Nations agency, which is viewed as key in determining the status of Iran's nuclear stockpile.

title: Trump, JD Vance Call Iran Deal A Payday For US Farmers, Tehran Denies It
content: Donald Trump and JD Vance say that any frozen funds and assets held outside of Iran will be used to buy US crops.

title: No Kennedy In Congress Next Year As Jack Schlossberg Loses New York Race
content: The campaign was colourful and hotly contested, partly because of Schlossberg's star power as the social-media-savvy grandson of the late President John F Kennedy, but also because the race became an expensive proxy fight among artificial intelligence interests.

title: Barnacles, Algae And Delays: The Cleanup Challenge As Hormuz Gradually Reopens
content: Barnacles, algae and mussels are creating fresh delays for ships waiting to resume journeys through one of the world's busiest oil routes

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 600 Points Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude futures were at $76.71 a barrel, and US WTI slipped 36 cents to $72.85 a barrel.

title: Only 4 States Roll Out Centre's New Labour Codes: Corporates Scramble For Clarity
content: What was intended as a simplification exercise at the national level is often turning into an operational challenge on the ground.

title: Rs 51 Crore Lost, Only Rs 55 Lakh Recovered: Report Flags Kerala Distillery Gaps
content: As per the CAG audit, former Kerala Excise Minister MB Rajesh treated it as routine wastage and recovered only a fraction.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Meta Makes A $900 Million Super-App Move In India
content: Cred's founder and Chief Executive Officer Kunal Shah will move to Meta as the head of its flagship messaging service.

title: Wall Collapses, Trees Uprooted, Car Crushed: Mumbai Monsoon Rain Mayhem
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued orange alert for Mumbai, warning of moderate to heavy rain accompanied by thunder and lightning.

title: How A Hoodie In 33 Degrees Celsius Heat Led Cops To Pune Man's Killers
content: Siya and Ketan - the young son of a prominent Maharashtra businessman - had announced their engagement in February, and a grand wedding involving private jets and palaces was scheduled for November.

title: SpaceX Sells $25 Billion In Bonds To Cut Elon Musk's Interest Burden
content: Ahead of SpaceX's historic $75 billion initial public offering earlier this month, Elon Musk fused many of his disparate companies into a conglomerate that investors gushed over, even though it doesn't yet make a profit.

title: Gautam Adani Unveils Big Bet On Nuclear Power, AI, Infrastructure
content: The Adani Group Chairman said the company is positioning itself for a world where energy security, technology and sovereignty are intertwined.

title: Read Full Text Of Gautam Adani's Speech At Adani AGM 2026
content: Gautam Adani said Adani Power is implementing India's largest ever private sector power capex programme of over Rs 2 lakh crore.

title: Canada, Ireland Renew Anti-Terror Resolve On 41 Years Of 1985 Bombing
content: The bombing of Flight 182 remains one of the worst terrorist attacks linked to Canada and the deadliest incident in Air India's history.

title: Bengaluru Woman, 75, Beaten To Death By Alcohol-Addicted Daughter, Grandson
content: A 75-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her daughter and grandson over a property dispute in Bengaluru.

title: Bengaluru Metro Purple Line services resume; technical snag resolved after hours of disruption
content: Regular operations resumed from 5 a.m. after a technical issue that had severely affected services the previous evening was successfully rectified, the BMRCL said

title: Experience centre for home owners in Belagavi
content: The 850 sq. ft. experience centre showcases modular kitchens, wardrobes, and complete home interior solutions

title: Golden jubilee celebrations of Gulbarga University Microbiology Department starting on June 25
content: The celebrations will begin with a two-day national conference focusing on emerging microbial technologies and their role in shaping a sustainable future

title: IMD forecasts a wet weekend for Andhra Pradesh
content: All the three regions, South and North Coastal A.P. and Rayalaseema, may receive light to moderate showers till the end of the month

title: BRICS can play a key role in tackling terrorism: PM
content: ‘India’s chairmanship of BRICS will help in enhancing priorities of the Global South and contribute to a safer world’

title: BWSSB chairperson directs officials to prepare for possible water scarcity in Bengaluru
content: BWSSB Chairperson N. Manjula held detailed discussions with officials on the current water levels in the Krishna Raja Sagara (KRS) reservoir and emphasised the need for its prudent utilisation

title: Congress holds ‘Asud Morcha’ in Nanded; calls out PM Modi, CM Fadnavis for failing to address farmers’ issues
content: Maharashtra Congress President took a potshot at CM Fadnavis, calling him a “so-called divine power”, who is unable to resolve the problems of farmers, but able to break opposition parties

title: Name, content of NCERT class 6 Kannada textbook turn contentious
content: The recent release of the NCERT class 6 R3 Kannada textbook, controversially titled “Krishna,” has sparked widespread criticism from educationists and writers in Bengaluru.

title: El Nino effect: Centre says 111 districts with ‘poor irrigation facilities’ are a concern
content: The Centre has assessed that 315 districts are vulnerable and 111 districts in 12 States are of primary concern due to poor irrigation facilities

title: HC bars GIDA from installing kiosks on Queen’s Walkway
content: Commercial kiosks on the walkway would impede pedestrian movement and affect fundamental rights, says court

title: Commuters stranded for hours as technical fault disrupts Bengaluru Metro Purple Line services
content: Due to the disruption, train services were operated only between Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Station (Majestic) and Challaghatta on one side and between Indiranagar and Whitefield (Kadugodi) on the other

title: Source of infection of Nipah patient in Kozhikode remains unclear
content: According to official sources in the Animal Husbandry department, samples of dead fruit bats, bird droppings, and body fluid samples of some domestic animals were collected from within a five-km radius of the residential premises of the infected person

title: Two arrested in Gurugram over ‘forged reports’ in Punjab CM Mann video row
content: Punjab leaders defend independent forensic reports cited by the AAP, Akal Takht calls the alleged forgery a grave matter, urges Haryana Police to expose everyone involved.

title: CM Vijay tells ‘short story’ in Assembly; Udhayanidhi says House turned into a cinema
content: Though the CM did not name anyone from the Opposition parties in his story, his comments were widely interpreted as a reference to the defeat of former CM and DMK president M.K. Stalin in Kolathur

title: Congress slams MP CM over 'land deals', Akhilesh sees 'BJP plot' to topple 3 CMs
content: BJP's 'double-engine' government in Madhya Pradesh, 'engine of plunder' is running at full speed . akhilesh Yadav alleged that land-related accusations against CM were part of a BJP plot to discredit and replace 3 of its CMs, including in Rajasthan and UP .

title: 'Looting in name of Ram': Baghel attacks govt over schools, farmers and Ram temple donations
content: ex-chhattisgarh chief minister launches blistering attack on the centre . he accuses the RSS, VHP and Bajrang Dal of "looting in the name of Ram" the government is playing a cruel joke on the farmers, he says .

title: 'Anti-Hindu, anti-desi hate': Indian-American lawmakers urge diaspora to enter politics
